description_long: This is an electric guitar with a classic double-cutaway body finished in a sunburst gradient, transitioning from a deep brown at the edges to a lighter amber in the center. The body features two metallic pickups, four gold control knobs, and a fixed bridge, all mounted on the front face. The neck is long and slender, with a dark fretboard and metallic frets, leading up to a headstock equipped with six tuning pegs arranged three on each side. The back of the guitar is a solid brown color with two access panels, likely for electronics and the tremolo system. The overall construction appears to be primarily wood with metallic hardware and plastic components for the knobs and pickup covers. This instrument is typical of those found in urban music venues, street performances, or garage bands.
description: A sunburst-finished electric guitar with a double-cutaway body, metallic pickups, gold knobs, and a dark fretboard, featuring a classic headstock with six tuning pegs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sunburst finish, pickups, control knobs, and bridge.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the neck, headstock, and the three-per-side tuning peg arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid brown back and two access panels.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizing the guitar's body contour and the back of the headstock.
attribute_headstock_design: Classic 3+3 tuning peg layout, no visible logo, standard contoured shape
